channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: FutureSwell Creator Lab 2026: A Global Accelerator Empowering Ocean Storytellers to Drive Climate Action
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The urgency of ocean conservation has never been greater—and neither has the power of storytelling. Recognizing the influence of digital creators in shaping public opinion and mobilizing communities, FutureSwell has launched its inaugural Creator Lab 2026, an 8-week virtual accelerator designed to equip ocean-focused creators with the tools, mentorship, and strategic clarity needed to drive […]

The post FutureSwell Creator Lab 2026: A Global Accelerator Empowering Ocean Storytellers to Drive Climate Action appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The urgency of ocean conservation has never been greater—and neither has the power of storytelling. Recognizing the influence of digital creators in shaping public opinion and mobilizing communities, FutureSwell has launched its inaugural Creator Lab 2026, an 8-week virtual accelerator designed to equip ocean-focused creators with the tools, mentorship, and strategic clarity needed to drive real-world impact.

This program is more than just a training experience—it is a movement to amplify voices, align narratives, and catalyze collective action for the future of our oceans.

About FutureSwell

FutureSwell is a global storytelling platform and consultancy founded in 2020 with a mission to increase public engagement in ocean climate issues. Through modern media, partnerships, and community-building initiatives, FutureSwell works to:

  • Amplify ocean-based solutions
  • Elevate diverse community voices
  • Advocate for systemic environmental change

By combining storytelling with strategic advocacy, FutureSwell creates accessible pathways for individuals from all backgrounds to participate in ocean conservation efforts.

What is the Creator Lab?

The FutureSwell Creator Lab is an intensive, fully virtual, and free 8-week accelerator program aimed at empowering ocean conservation content creators. It focuses on transforming passionate storytellers into impactful campaigners who can shape narratives and influence global conversations around ocean climate issues.

Participants will engage in a structured learning journey that blends education, mentorship, and hands-on project development.

Program Highlights

Throughout the 2-month incubator, selected creators will:

1. Develop a Narrative Project

  • Create and publish a storytelling project aligned with a specific ocean climate campaign focus
  • Collaborate with FutureSwell to ensure strategic impact and visibility

2. Receive Expert Mentorship

  • Learn directly from leaders in ocean conservation, media, and campaigning
  • Gain insights into effective communication strategies and audience engagement

3. Build Campaign Strategy Skills

  • Understand how to align personal storytelling with broader environmental goals
  • Learn how to mobilize audiences and influence public perception

4. Join a Global Community

  • Connect with like-minded ocean storytellers from around the world
  • Become part of a collaborative network committed to long-term impact

5. Gain Long-Term Clarity

  • Define your role within the global ocean conservation movement
  • Leave the program with a clear direction for future content and campaigns

Learning Experience

The Creator Lab combines multiple learning formats to ensure a comprehensive experience:

  • Live training seminars
  • Interactive workshops
  • One-on-one and group mentorship
  • Peer collaboration and feedback
  • Real-world project execution

This hybrid approach ensures that participants not only learn but also apply their knowledge in meaningful ways.

Who Should Apply?

FutureSwell is looking for creators who are:

  • Passionate about ocean conservation and climate issues
  • Active on social media or digital platforms
  • Interested in using storytelling as a tool for change
  • Eager to grow their skills in campaigning and communication
  • Committed to contributing to a collective mission

No specific professional background is required—FutureSwell believes conservation is for everyone.

What You Will Gain

By the end of the program, participants will:

  • Have a published narrative project with real-world impact
  • Gain advanced storytelling and campaign strategy skills
  • Build connections with global experts and peers
  • Strengthen their personal brand as an ocean advocate
  • Be equipped to drive systemic change through content

Application Details

  • Program Duration: 8 weeks (virtual)
  • Cost: Free
  • Application Deadline: May 15, 2026
  • Review Process: Rolling basis (applications may close early once spots are filled)

Why This Matters

In today’s digital age, creators are not just influencers—they are changemakers. They shape conversations, influence culture, and mobilize communities. The FutureSwell Creator Lab recognizes this power and seeks to harness it for one of the most critical causes of our time: protecting our oceans.

By training and supporting creators, FutureSwell is building a global network of storytellers who can drive awareness, inspire action, and contribute to a sustainable future.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the FutureSwell Creator Lab 2026.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post FutureSwell Creator Lab 2026: A Global Accelerator Empowering Ocean Storytellers to Drive Climate Action appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Caribbean Development Bank Young Professionals Programme 2026–2028 (Fully Paid)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Caribbean Development Bank (CDB) has officially opened applications for its Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026–2028—a highly competitive, fully paid opportunity for early-career professionals looking to build a global career in international development. This prestigious programme offers a two-year contract in Barbados, hands-on experience across key development sectors, and the chance to work with one […]

The post Caribbean Development Bank Young Professionals Programme 2026–2028 (Fully Paid) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Caribbean Development Bank (CDB) has officially opened applications for its Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026–2028—a highly competitive, fully paid opportunity for early-career professionals looking to build a global career in international development.

This prestigious programme offers a two-year contract in Barbados, hands-on experience across key development sectors, and the chance to work with one of the Caribbean’s leading multilateral institutions focused on sustainable growth and resilience.

About the CDB Young Professionals Programme

The CDB Young Professionals Programme is designed to recruit and develop high-potential young professionals (under 32 years old) with strong academic backgrounds and relevant work experience.

Participants gain exposure to the Bank’s operations, policies, and development initiatives while contributing to impactful regional projects. The programme typically runs for two years, offering structured learning, real-world assignments, and career progression opportunities .

Available Roles for 2026–2028 Cohort

For the 2026 intake, CDB is recruiting four young professionals across the following areas:

  • Human Resources Data Analyst
    (Human Resource Management, Data Analytics, Statistics, Business Administration, Information Systems, Economics, or related fields)
  • Digital Communications
    (Communications, Media Production, Digital Media, Journalism, Graphic Design, or related fields)
  • Data Analytics and Information Management
    (Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Data Science, or related disciplines)
  • Private Sector Development
    (Economics, Finance, Business Administration, International Development, Public Policy, or related fields)

Salary and Benefits

Successful candidates will receive a competitive international package, including:

  • Starting salary: USD $60,600 per year
  • 6 weeks paid annual leave
  • Comprehensive medical insurance
  • Full relocation support (including family relocation)
  • Placement at CDB Headquarters in Barbados

This aligns with global standards for multilateral development banks, where young professionals programmes often combine strong compensation with structured career development pathways.

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, candidates must meet the following requirements:

  • Be under 32 years old by December 31, 2026
  • Be a national of a CDB member country
  • Hold a relevant degree (typically at least a master’s level)
  • Have a minimum of 2 years of relevant professional experience
  • Demonstrate strong interest in international development and regional impact

The programme targets candidates ready to transition into impactful careers within development institutions.

Eligible Countries

The Caribbean Development Bank (CDB) has 28 member countries, and you must be a national of one of these to apply for programmes like the Young Professionals Programme.

Here’s the full list:

 CDB Member Countries

🇦🇮 Anguilla
🇦🇬 Antigua and Barbuda
🇧🇸 Bahamas
🇧🇧 Barbados
🇧🇿 Belize
🇧🇷 Brazil
🇻🇬 British Virgin Islands
🇨🇦 Canada
🇰🇾 Cayman Islands
🇨🇳 China
🇨🇴 Colombia
🇩🇲 Dominica
🇩🇪 Germany
🇬🇩 Grenada
🇬🇾 Guyana
🇭🇹 Haiti
🇮🇹 Italy
🇯🇲 Jamaica
🇲🇽 Mexico
🇲🇸 Montserrat
🇱🇨 Saint Lucia
🇰🇳 Saint Kitts and Nevis
🇻🇨 Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
🇸🇷 Suriname
🇹🇹 Trinidad and Tobago
🇹🇨 Turks and Caicos Islands
🇬🇧 United Kingdom
🇻🇪 Venezuela

Why You Should Apply

The CDB Young Professionals Programme is one of the top entry routes into international development careers, offering:

  • Direct experience in a multilateral development bank
  • Exposure to regional policy, finance, and project implementation
  • Opportunity to work on youth development, climate resilience, and economic growth
  • A strong pathway into long-term international careers

CDB places a strong emphasis on youth empowerment and capacity building, recognizing young professionals as key drivers of sustainable development across the Caribbean .

Application Deadline

  • Deadline: May 15, 2026

👉 Apply here: https://career5.successfactors.eu/career?company=caribbeandP2&career_ns=job_listing_summary

👉  Find out more here https://www.caribank.org/work-with-us/careers

Young Professional, Digital Communications

Application Deadline
Friday, May 15, 2026 – 23:59

The role spans digital content development, video and audio production, graphic design, and cross‑platform storytelling and event production support. It ensures that high‑quality, brand‑aligned content is created for internal and external audiences across websites, social media, digital campaigns, and multimedia channels.

Young Professional, Human Resources Data Analyst

Application Deadline
Friday, May 15, 2026 – 23:59

The role assists in collecting, analysing, and interpreting HR data from multiple sources to support workforce planning, talent management, and organisational performance monitoring. The incumbent will also contribute to the development of HR dashboards, reports, and data visualisations that provide timely insights into workforce metrics and trends, spanning the breadth of the HRD.

Young Professional, Private Sector

Application Deadline
Friday, May 15, 2026 – 23:59

The role is designed to provide broad exposure to private sector development operations and policy work, enabling the YP to contribute meaningfully to CDB’s strategic objectives while developing professional expertise in investment finance, project management, and development banking.

Young Professional, Data Analytics and Information Management

Application Deadline
Friday, May 15, 2026 – 23:59

The Young Professional (YP), Data Analytics and Information Management role supports the Bank’s data analytics and information governance work by helping develop dashboards and reports, collecting/validating data, maintaining KPI and metadata documentation, and contributing to data governance artifacts (e.g., data dictionaries and glossaries). The YP also coordinates meetings and training, completes progress updates and research on improved tools and practices, and provides first-level support to users while building professional capability through on-the-job learning and mentoring.

Pro Tip

Tailor your application to demonstrate:

  • Strong analytical or technical skills relevant to the role
  • Clear interest in Caribbean or global development issues
  • Evidence of impact, leadership, or problem-solving in your past work

Multilateral institutions look for candidates who combine technical expertise with a clear development mindset—so make that explicit in your CV and cover letter.

Final Thoughts

If you’re looking to break into global development, policy, or international finance, the Caribbean Development Bank Young Professionals Programme 2026–2028 is a high-impact, career-defining opportunity.

With competitive benefits, global exposure, and meaningful work, this programme offers a powerful launchpad into the world of international development.

The post Caribbean Development Bank Young Professionals Programme 2026–2028 (Fully Paid) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: New Development Bank Internship Programme 2026 (Shanghai, China) – Apply by June 30
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Are you looking to build a career in international development, finance, or sustainable infrastructure? The New Development Bank Internship Programme 2026 is now open for applications, offering a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in a global, multicultural environment. This prestigious internship is based in Shanghai, China, and is open to young professionals from across […]

The post New Development Bank Internship Programme 2026 (Shanghai, China) – Apply by June 30 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you looking to build a career in international development, finance, or sustainable infrastructure? The New Development Bank Internship Programme 2026 is now open for applications, offering a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in a global, multicultural environment.

This prestigious internship is based in Shanghai, China, and is open to young professionals from across NDB member countries.

Key Summary

    • Host Organization: New Development Bank
    • Programme: Internship Programme 2026
    • Location: Shanghai, China
    • Deadline: June 30, 2026 (11:59 PM CST)
    • Eligibility: Nationals of NDB member countries, aged 30 or below
    • Experience Level: Maximum 1 year of full-time work experience
    • Education: Master’s completed within last 12 months OR currently enrolled
    • Language: English (required)

 

About the New Development Bank (NDB)

The New Development Bank (NDB) is a multilateral development bank established by the BRICS countries—Brazil, Russia, India, China, and South Africa. Its mission is to mobilize resources for infrastructure and sustainable development projects across emerging markets and developing countries (EMDCs).

Through its internship programme, NDB aims to nurture the next generation of global development professionals.

NDB Member Countries

To be eligible, applicants must be nationals of one of the NDB member countries:

  • Brazil
  • Russia
  • India
  • China
  • South Africa
  • Bangladesh
  • United Arab Emirates
  • Egypt

Note: Membership may expand—always confirm on the official portal.

Internship Overview

  • Location: Shanghai, China
  • Programme Type: Full-time Internship
  • Target Applicants: Nationals of NDB member countries
  • Application Deadline: June 30, 2026 (11:59 PM China Standard Time)

What You’ll Do (Key Responsibilities)

As an NDB intern, you will gain real-world experience by supporting teams across different departments. Your responsibilities may include:

  • Providing administrative support (data entry, scheduling, documentation)
  • Conducting research and data analysis
  • Assisting with projects and departmental initiatives
  • Supporting report writing and documentation
  • Participating in team meetings and presentations
  • Engaging in training and professional development
  • Collaborating with cross-functional teams
  • Supporting presentations and stakeholder materials
  • Handling ad hoc assignments and special tasks

Responsibilities may vary depending on the department.

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the NDB Internship Programme 2026, you must:

  • Be a national of an NDB member country
  • Be 30 years old or younger
  • Have fluency in English
  • Have no more than 1 year of full-time work experience
  • Have:
    • Completed a postgraduate (Master’s) degree within the last 12 months, OR
    • Be currently enrolled in a full-time postgraduate programme
  • Be available to work full-time during the internship

Why Apply? (Benefits)

  • Work at a leading multilateral development bank
  • Collaborate with international experts
  • Gain experience in development finance and infrastructure
  • Build strong research, analytical, and professional skills
  • Strengthen your pathway into UN, World Bank, and global careers

Life at the New Development Bank

NDB offers a dynamic, diverse, and forward-looking work environment:

  • Innovative: Encourages new ideas and fresh thinking
  • Diverse: Inclusive culture with global talent
  • Forward-Looking: Focused on sustainable development impact

How to Apply

Ready to apply? Use the official link below:

👉 Apply Here: https://career15.sapsf.cn/career?career%5fns=job%5flisting&company=newdevelop&navBarLevel=JOB%5fSEARCH&rcm%5fsite%5flocale=en%5fUS&career_job_req_id=1780&selected_lang=en_US

🔗 Explore our careers page for more details: www.ndb.int/careers

📌 Note: Applicants based in Russia experiencing technical issues can send their CV and cover letter to career@ndb.int

Pro Tip for Applicants

To stand out:

  • Highlight coursework or research in development, finance, or economics
  • Showcase analytical, research, and communication skills
  • Include relevant academic projects, internships, or volunteer work

Final Thoughts

The New Development Bank Internship Programme 2026 is a high-impact global opportunity for young graduates ready to step into international development and finance. If you meet the criteria, this is one opportunity you don’t want to miss.

For more internships and global opportunities:
Visit Opportunities for Youth https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=Intern

The post New Development Bank Internship Programme 2026 (Shanghai, China) – Apply by June 30 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship 2026–2027: Complete Guide to Eligibility, Application Process, Research Focus & How to Apply
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE), through its Cañada Blanch Centre, is inviting applications for the highly regarded LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship for the Autumn Term 2026–2027. This prestigious academic opportunity is designed for scholars seeking to deepen their research, collaborate internationally, and engage with one of the world’s leading social science institutions. […]

The post LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship 2026–2027: Complete Guide to Eligibility, Application Process, Research Focus & How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE), through its Cañada Blanch Centre, is inviting applications for the highly regarded LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship for the Autumn Term 2026–2027.

This prestigious academic opportunity is designed for scholars seeking to deepen their research, collaborate internationally, and engage with one of the world’s leading social science institutions. If you are an academic, PhD researcher, or established scholar working on globally relevant topics, this fellowship provides a valuable platform to advance your work within a dynamic intellectual environment.

What is the LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship?

The LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship is a short-term academic residency program hosted at the Cañada Blanch Centre at LSE in London. It offers selected fellows the opportunity to:

  • Conduct independent research
  • Access LSE’s world-class academic resources
  • Engage with scholars and researchers across disciplines
  • Contribute to ongoing academic discussions and initiatives

The fellowship is highly selective, with only a small cohort of 3 to 4 fellows admitted each year, ensuring a focused and high-impact academic experience.

Fellowship Duration and Location

  • Program Period: Monday, 28 September – Friday, 11 December 2026
  • Location: London, United Kingdom
  • Host Institution: London School of Economics and Political Science

During this period, fellows will be embedded within the academic ecosystem of LSE, benefiting from its global reputation in research and policy influence.

Important Deadline

  • Application Deadline: 1 June 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their materials well in advance to ensure full consideration.

What Does the Fellowship Offer?

While the LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship is not a funded program, it provides substantial academic and professional benefits:

1. Access to World-Class Research Facilities

  • Full access to LSE libraries and academic resources
  • Exposure to cutting-edge research tools and data

2. Academic Collaboration Opportunities

  • Interaction with leading scholars and researchers
  • Participation in seminars, lectures, and academic events

3. International Research Exposure

  • Opportunity to conduct research in a globally recognized institution
  • Build cross-border academic networks

4. Prestige and Academic Recognition

  • Enhance your academic profile by affiliating with LSE
  • Strengthen your research credibility and visibility

Funding Information

It is important to note that:

  • The fellowship does not provide financial support
  • Selected fellows must secure funding independently through:
    • Home institutions
    • External grants
    • Personal resources

This makes it essential for applicants to plan their finances before applying.

Research Areas and Priority Topics

The fellowship welcomes a broad range of research topics, particularly those aligned with global challenges and policy-relevant issues. Preference is given to proposals addressing:

  1. Sustainable Development
  2. Climate Change and Environmental Policy
  3. Inequality and Social Justice
  4. Demographic Change and Migration
  5. Innovation, Technology, and Productivity
  6. Governance, Democracy, and Public Policy
  7. European Integration and Regional Cooperation
  8. Education and Employment Trends
  9. Public Health Challenges
  10. Gender and Ethnic Inequality

Additionally, projects that bridge research between Spain and the United Kingdom are particularly encouraged.

Who Can Apply?

The fellowship is open to a wide range of academic professionals, including:

  • Early-career researchers
  • PhD candidates
  • Established academics and professors

Applicants should be actively engaged in research relevant to the Centre’s thematic focus areas.

Application Requirements

To apply, candidates must submit the following:

Required Documents

  1. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  2. Research Proposal (Maximum 600 Words)

Your research proposal should clearly outline:

  • Your research topic and objectives
  • Methodology
  • Expected outcomes
  • Relevance to the Centre’s focus areas

How to Apply

Follow these steps to complete your application:

Step 1: Prepare Your Documents

Ensure your CV and research proposal are clear, concise, and aligned with the fellowship priorities.

Step 2: Submit via Email

Send your application to:
m.osuna-vergara@lse.ac.uk

Step 3: Use the Correct Subject Line

Subject: LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship Application

Step 4: Review Guidelines

Applicants are encouraged to review detailed guidelines available on the official LSE webpage before submission.

Selection Process

The selection committee evaluates applications based on:

  • Academic excellence
  • Relevance of research topic
  • Potential impact of the research
  • Alignment with the Centre’s mission
  • Contribution to academic discourse

Due to the limited number of fellowships available, competition is expected to be high.

Why You Should Apply

The LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Advance your research in a globally respected institution
  • Build international academic collaborations
  • Gain access to top-tier research infrastructure
  • Enhance your academic and professional trajectory

For scholars looking to position themselves at the forefront of global policy and research discussions, this fellowship is a strategic opportunity.

Final Thoughts

The LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship 2026–2027 stands out as a prestigious academic residency designed for researchers committed to addressing some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

Although it does not provide direct funding, the intellectual, institutional, and networking benefits make it an invaluable opportunity for serious academics aiming to elevate their research and global impact.

If your research aligns with global policy issues and you are ready to engage with a world-class academic community, this fellowship is well worth pursuing.

For more information about this fellowship visit HERE.

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE

The post LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship 2026–2027: Complete Guide to Eligibility, Application Process, Research Focus & How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNICEF Philippines Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer Opportunity 2026 – Full-Time National Volunteer Role in Manila
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
If you are passionate about creating meaningful social impact, influencing positive behavioral change, and contributing to children’s welfare, this opportunity with UNICEF Philippines could be your next career breakthrough. The Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer role offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of communication, behavioral science, and development programming while supporting […]

The post UNICEF Philippines Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer Opportunity 2026 – Full-Time National Volunteer Role in Manila appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about creating meaningful social impact, influencing positive behavioral change, and contributing to children’s welfare, this opportunity with UNICEF Philippines could be your next career breakthrough. The Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer role offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of communication, behavioral science, and development programming while supporting national priorities and global Sustainable Development Goals.

Overview of the Opportunity

The Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer position is a full-time, onsite national UN Volunteer assignment based in Manila, Philippines. This 12-month role (with the possibility of extension) is designed for professionals eager to support UNICEF’s mission of promoting and protecting children’s rights, especially for the most vulnerable populations.

  • Location: Manila, Philippines
  • Start Date: 25 May 2026
  • Duration: 12 months (extendable)
  • Work Schedule: Full-time
  • Volunteer Type: National Specialist (UN Volunteer)
  • Work Mode: Onsite (UN premises)
  • Application Deadline: 12 May 2026

About UNICEF’s Mission and SBC Approach

UNICEF works globally to ensure every child thrives, focusing on health, education, protection, and equality. A key strategy in achieving these outcomes is Social and Behavior Change (SBC), which aims to:

  • Strengthen community engagement
  • Improve service uptake
  • Influence positive behaviors and social norms
  • Address barriers related to knowledge, attitudes, and practices

The SBC Officer will play a central role in advancing these objectives by supporting evidence-based strategies and fostering collaboration across sectors.

Key Responsibilities and Duties

The role is structured across five major functional areas:

1. Capacity Development for SBC

  • Facilitate learning programs and training sessions for UNICEF staff and partners
  • Conduct training needs assessments and evaluate learning outcomes
  • Maintain detailed documentation and reporting of capacity-building activities

2. Technical Support to Programme Sectors

  • Provide strategic and operational SBC support across sectors such as health, education, and child protection
  • Assist in designing, implementing, and monitoring SBC interventions
  • Conduct quality assurance reviews of program materials and plans

3. SBC Systems Strengthening and Integration

  • Support policy dialogues and multi-stakeholder engagement
  • Contribute to integrating SBC into academic and professional learning systems
  • Prepare concept notes, reports, and meeting summaries

4. Knowledge Management and Network Engagement

  • Develop knowledge products such as case studies, reports, and presentations
  • Support participation in conferences and workshops
  • Manage and organize SBC knowledge repositories

5. Monitoring, Reporting, and Programme Learning

  • Track program results and compile reports
  • Analyze data to improve program effectiveness
  • Document lessons learned for continuous improvement

Volunteerism and Additional Contributions

As a UN Volunteer, you are also expected to:

  • Promote volunteerism and community engagement
  • Participate in UNV events such as International Volunteer Day
  • Contribute articles and reports on field experiences
  • Support onboarding of new UN Volunteers
  • Encourage the use of online volunteering platforms

Required Qualifications and Experience

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in fields such as:
    • Development Communication
    • Behavioral Science
    • Sociology, Psychology, Anthropology
    • Communication for Development or related disciplines
  • Advanced degrees are an added advantage

Experience

  • Minimum of 3 years in:
    • Social and Behavior Change
    • Communication for Development
    • Program management or related fields
  • Experience in emergency or humanitarian settings is preferred

Languages

  • Fluent in English and Filipino

Core Competencies

Successful candidates will demonstrate:

  • Strong technical knowledge of SBC strategies
  • High ethical standards and integrity
  • Excellent teamwork and respect for diversity
  • Commitment to continuous learning
  • Strong planning, communication, and analytical skills
  • Flexibility and adaptability in dynamic environments

Benefits and Entitlements

As a National UN Volunteer, you will receive:

  • Monthly Volunteer Living Allowance (VLA)
  • Entry and Exit Lump Sum Allowances
  • Comprehensive insurance coverage
  • Annual leave (2.5 days per month)
  • Access to UN learning and career development resources

Living Conditions

Volunteers are responsible for arranging their own accommodation and daily living needs. Manila offers access to essential services including healthcare, markets, and communication infrastructure.

Important Notes

  • UNICEF is an equal opportunity organization committed to diversity and inclusion
  • Reasonable accommodations are available for persons with disabilities
  • COVID-19 vaccination is required for onsite roles
  • No fees are charged at any stage of the recruitment process

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply through the official UN Volunteer platform before the deadline. Ensure your application highlights relevant experience, technical skills, and alignment with UNICEF’s mission.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Volunteering Opportunities on OFY here.

The post UNICEF Philippines Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer Opportunity 2026 – Full-Time National Volunteer Role in Manila appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: USDA LAMP Grants 2026: Complete Guide to Local Agriculture Market Program Funding, Eligibility, Deadlines & How to Apply
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA), through its Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS), offers one of the most comprehensive funding opportunities for farmers, agribusinesses, and food system innovators: the Local Agriculture Market Program (LAMP). If you are looking to expand your agricultural business, strengthen local food systems, or access funding for value-added production, this in-depth guide […]

The post USDA LAMP Grants 2026: Complete Guide to Local Agriculture Market Program Funding, Eligibility, Deadlines & How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA), through its Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS), offers one of the most comprehensive funding opportunities for farmers, agribusinesses, and food system innovators: the Local Agriculture Market Program (LAMP).

If you are looking to expand your agricultural business, strengthen local food systems, or access funding for value-added production, this in-depth guide will walk you through everything you need to know about LAMP—its structure, funding streams, benefits, and how to apply successfully.

What is the Local Agriculture Market Program (LAMP)?

The Local Agriculture Market Program (LAMP) is a federally funded initiative established under the 2018 Farm Bill to support the development and expansion of:

  • Direct-to-consumer agricultural marketing
  • Local and regional food markets
  • Food enterprises and distribution systems
  • Value-added agricultural products

LAMP acts as an umbrella program, consolidating multiple grant opportunities designed to build resilient, efficient, and profitable food systems across the United States.

Primary Goals of the LAMP Program

The program is structured to deliver measurable impact across the agricultural ecosystem through the following objectives:

1. Strengthening Regional Food Economies

  • Promote collaboration between public and private sector stakeholders
  • Encourage partnerships that enhance food system resilience

2. Supporting Business Development

  • Fund feasibility studies and strategic planning
  • Enable producers to build scalable and sustainable operations

3. Expanding Value-Added Agriculture

  • Support innovation in food processing, packaging, and branding
  • Increase profitability through product diversification

4. Enhancing Food System Infrastructure

  • Improve storage, aggregation, and distribution networks
  • Strengthen supply chain efficiency

5. Increasing Economic Opportunities

  • Boost income for farmers and food businesses
  • Create employment opportunities in local communities

6. Simplifying Grant Processes

  • Streamline application procedures
  • Improve reporting and compliance systems

LAMP Funding Opportunities (Grant Programs Explained)

LAMP funding is distributed across four major grant programs. Each serves a distinct purpose within the agricultural value chain:

1. Farmers Market Promotion Program (FMPP)

Focus:

  • Direct-to-consumer marketing

Supports:

  • Farmers markets
  • Roadside stands
  • Community-supported agriculture (CSA)

Apply Here

Deadlines:
Deadlines vary annually. Applicants should check the official page for current funding cycles.

2. Local Food Promotion Program (LFPP)

Focus:

  • Local and regional food business development

Supports:

  • Food hubs
  • Aggregation and distribution systems
  • Value chain coordination

Apply Here

Deadlines:
Deadlines vary depending on funding announcements.

3. Regional Food System Partnerships Program (RFSP)

Focus:

  • Multi-stakeholder regional collaboration

Supports:

  • Partnerships between producers, businesses, and institutions
  • Regional food system planning and coordination

Apply Here


Deadlines:
Application timelines differ by funding cycle.

4. Value-Added Producer Grants (VAPG)

Administered by:
USDA Rural Development

Focus:

  • Increasing product value and marketability

Supports:

  • Processing and packaging
  • Branding and marketing strategies
  • Product innovation

Apply Here

Deadlines:
Deadlines vary and are announced per funding round.

Who Should Apply for LAMP Grants?

LAMP is designed for a broad range of stakeholders within the agricultural and food systems sector:

  • Farmers and agricultural producers
  • Food entrepreneurs and agribusiness owners
  • Cooperatives and producer groups
  • Nonprofit organizations
  • Food system networks and alliances
  • Public-private partnerships

Key Benefits of the LAMP Program

Participating in LAMP provides both financial and strategic advantages:

1. Access to Federal Funding

  • Grants to support business growth and innovation
  • Funding for infrastructure and operational improvements

2. Market Expansion Opportunities

  • Improved access to local and regional markets
  • Enhanced consumer reach

3. Strengthened Capacity

  • Better operational efficiency
  • Improved supply chain systems

4. Community and Economic Impact

  • Job creation
  • Increased local economic activity

5. Long-Term Sustainability

  • Support for environmentally and economically sustainable practices

Additional Resources for Applicants

To help applicants succeed, USDA provides several support tools:

  • LAMP Grant Toolkits & Application Support
  • LAMP Infographic (PDF)
  • LAMP Report to Congress
  • Seeds of Success Series (case studies and success stories)
  • LAMP Navigator Tool (explore funded projects and impact)
  • FY25 LAMP Webinar Recording

These resources provide guidance on application preparation, project design, and implementation.

How to Apply for LAMP Grants

Follow these steps to submit a strong application:

Step 1: Identify the Right Program

Choose the grant that aligns with your business or project goals.

Step 2: Review Guidelines

Carefully read eligibility criteria and application instructions.

Step 3: Prepare Your Proposal

Include:

  • Business plan or project outline
  • Budget and financial projections
  • Expected outcomes and impact

Step 4: Use Available Resources

Leverage USDA toolkits and webinars to strengthen your application.

Step 5: Submit Your Application

Apply through the official USDA grant portals linked above.

Important Notes on Deadlines

There is no single universal deadline for the LAMP program.
Each grant program (FMPP, LFPP, RFSP, VAPG) has its own application timeline.

Applicants are advised to:

  • Regularly check the official USDA website
  • Monitor funding announcements
  • Apply early within open cycles

Why LAMP Matters

The Local Agriculture Market Program plays a critical role in transforming the agricultural landscape by:

  • Strengthening food security
  • Supporting small and mid-sized producers
  • Encouraging innovation in agriculture
  • Building resilient and sustainable food systems

It is a key driver of economic growth and sustainability within the U.S. agricultural sector.

Final Thoughts

The USDA LAMP Grants Program is more than just funding—it is a strategic investment in the future of agriculture. By supporting innovation, collaboration, and market expansion, LAMP empowers producers and organizations to thrive in an evolving global food system.

If you are ready to scale your agricultural impact, now is the time to explore and apply for LAMP funding opportunities.

For more information about this grant visit HERE

Discover more grant opportunities from OFY Here

 

The post USDA LAMP Grants 2026: Complete Guide to Local Agriculture Market Program Funding, Eligibility, Deadlines & How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OpenAI Safety Fellowship 2026: A Transformative Opportunity for AI Researchers to Shape the Future of Safe and Ethical AI
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence has brought unprecedented opportunities—and equally significant challenges. As AI systems grow more powerful, the importance of ensuring their safety, reliability, and ethical alignment becomes critical. Recognizing this, OpenAI has launched an exciting new initiative: the OpenAI Safety Fellowship. This pilot fellowship program is designed to support independent researchers, engineers, […]

The post OpenAI Safety Fellowship 2026: A Transformative Opportunity for AI Researchers to Shape the Future of Safe and Ethical AI appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence has brought unprecedented opportunities—and equally significant challenges. As AI systems grow more powerful, the importance of ensuring their safety, reliability, and ethical alignment becomes critical. Recognizing this, OpenAI has launched an exciting new initiative: the OpenAI Safety Fellowship.

This pilot fellowship program is designed to support independent researchers, engineers, and practitioners in conducting high-impact work focused on AI safety and alignment. If you are passionate about shaping the future of responsible AI, this opportunity could be a pivotal step in your career.

Overview of the OpenAI Safety Fellowship

The OpenAI Safety Fellowship is a structured program aimed at nurturing the next generation of experts in AI safety. It combines financial support, mentorship, and access to cutting-edge resources to empower fellows to produce meaningful research.

Key Program Details

  • Program Duration: September 14, 2026 – February 5, 2027
  • Application Deadline: May 3, 2026
  • Notification of Results: July 25, 2026
  • Work Mode:
    • In-person workspace available in Berkeley
    • Remote participation is also supported

Fellowship Objectives

The program focuses on advancing research that addresses critical safety challenges in modern and future AI systems. OpenAI is particularly interested in work that is:

  • Empirically grounded
  • Technically rigorous
  • Relevant to real-world AI systems
  • Valuable to the broader research community

Priority Research Areas

Applicants are encouraged to propose projects in key domains of AI safety and alignment. These include, but are not limited to:

  1. Safety Evaluation
    • Developing benchmarks and methods to assess AI system safety
  2. Ethics in AI
    • Addressing fairness, accountability, and societal impacts
  3. Robustness
    • Ensuring AI systems perform reliably under diverse conditions
  4. Scalable Mitigations
    • Designing solutions that scale with increasingly powerful AI systems
  5. Privacy-Preserving Safety Methods
    • Protecting user data while maintaining safety standards
  6. Agentic Oversight
    • Monitoring and controlling autonomous AI agents
  7. High-Severity Misuse Prevention
    • Reducing risks from malicious or unintended misuse of AI

What Fellows Will Receive

The OpenAI Safety Fellowship offers a comprehensive support system to ensure fellows can focus on impactful research.

Benefits Include:

  • Monthly Stipend
    • Financial support to cover living expenses
  • Compute Resources
    • Access to computational tools necessary for advanced AI research
  • API Credits
    • Resources to experiment and build using OpenAI technologies
  • Mentorship
    • Direct guidance from experienced researchers and professionals
  • Collaborative Environment
    • Engagement with a cohort of fellows and access to a shared workspace

Fellowship Expectations

Participants in the program are expected to:

  • Conduct rigorous and independent research
  • Collaborate with mentors and peers
  • Produce a substantial research output, such as:
    • A research paper
    • A benchmark
    • A dataset

This output should contribute meaningfully to the field of AI safety and alignment.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship welcomes individuals from a wide range of disciplines, emphasizing capability over credentials.

Relevant Backgrounds Include:

  • Computer Science
  • Social Sciences
  • Cybersecurity
  • Privacy Research
  • Human-Computer Interaction (HCI)
  • Related interdisciplinary fields

Selection Criteria:

  • Strong research ability
  • Sound technical judgment
  • Proven execution skills
  • Commitment to AI safety

Applicants must also provide reference contacts as part of the application.

Application Process

Interested candidates can Click here to know more and  to apply 

Important Dates Recap:

  • Applications Open: Already open
  • Deadline: May 3, 2026
  • Selection Notification: July 25, 2026

Why This Fellowship Matters

The OpenAI Safety Fellowship is more than just a research program—it is a strategic initiative to build a global community of experts dedicated to ensuring AI systems remain safe, ethical, and aligned with human values.

By participating, fellows gain:

  • Exposure to real-world AI challenges
  • Opportunities to influence the future of AI policy and development
  • A platform to publish impactful work
  • Connections with leading experts in the field

For more global fellowship opportunities Click here

The post OpenAI Safety Fellowship 2026: A Transformative Opportunity for AI Researchers to Shape the Future of Safe and Ethical AI appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Ford Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): Global Opportunity for Entrepreneurs and Community Leaders Driving Impact and Resilience
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Ford Philanthropy Fellowship 2026, powered by the Watson Institute, is a prestigious, fully funded global fellowship designed to empower entrepreneurs and community leaders who are building impactful solutions in areas such as community development, education, and disaster preparedness. This highly competitive program equips selected fellows with entrepreneurial training, mentorship, financial tools, and a global […]

The post Ford Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): Global Opportunity for Entrepreneurs and Community Leaders Driving Impact and Resilience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Ford Philanthropy Fellowship 2026, powered by the Watson Institute, is a prestigious, fully funded global fellowship designed to empower entrepreneurs and community leaders who are building impactful solutions in areas such as community development, education, and disaster preparedness.

This highly competitive program equips selected fellows with entrepreneurial training, mentorship, financial tools, and a global network, enabling them to scale their ventures and strengthen communities worldwide.

As detailed in the official program overview , the fellowship is tailored for leaders who are already making measurable impact and are ready to expand their reach and sustainability.

About the Ford Philanthropy Fellowship

The Ford Philanthropy Fellowship is an immersive leadership and entrepreneurship program designed to:

  • Support local changemakers addressing real-world challenges
  • Build resilient and thriving communities
  • Strengthen ventures focused on education, workforce development, and disaster response
  • Foster innovation and sustainable community impact

The fellowship combines structured learning, mentorship, and hands-on implementation to create a transformative experience for participants.

What the Fellowship Offers

The program delivers a comprehensive set of benefits aimed at accelerating entrepreneurial growth and leadership development.

1. Business & Leadership Workshops

Fellows participate in hands-on workshops covering:

  • Entrepreneurship and business development
  • Financial literacy and management
  • Strategic leadership and decision-making
  • Scaling impact-driven ventures

These sessions are led by experienced entrepreneurs and global experts.

2. Financial Tools and Resources

Participants gain access to:

  • Practical financial frameworks
  • Funding strategies and insights
  • Tools to strengthen venture sustainability
  • Guidance from financial experts and practitioners

This ensures fellows are equipped to manage and grow their ventures effectively.

3. Basecamps: Community Impact Initiatives

A unique feature of the fellowship is the Basecamp experience, where fellows:

  • Design and lead a 1-day community workshop
  • Support early-stage entrepreneurs
  • Strengthen local entrepreneurial ecosystems
  • Receive training and a stipend to implement the initiative

This ensures direct, localized impact within each fellow’s community.

4. Pitch Training and Virtual Summit

Fellows receive dedicated training to:

  • Develop compelling venture pitches
  • Refine storytelling and communication
  • Present their work to stakeholders and investors

The program culminates in a Virtual Summit (January 21, 2027) where fellows showcase their ventures.

5. Mentorship and Global Network

Participants benefit from:

  • One-on-one mentorship
  • Access to a global network of entrepreneurs and leaders
  • Long-term alumni support

This network continues to provide opportunities even after the fellowship ends.

Experts and Facilitators

Fellows learn directly from industry leaders and practitioners. Previous experts include:

  • Cole Hoover – Strategic Facilitator and Purpose Coach
  • Kimberly Moore – EdTech CEO and Education Strategist
  • Sarah Windrum – Business Leader and Innovation Expert

These experts provide both group training and personalized guidance.

Program Structure and Duration

The fellowship runs from:

  • Start Date: September 8, 2026
  • End Date: January 21, 2027

Key Components:

  • Virtual immersive experience
  • Weekly workshops (Tuesday–Thursday)
  • Mentorship sessions
  • Community-based Basecamp project
  • Final Summit presentation

Weekly Commitment:

  • Approximately 8–10 hours per week

Important Dates

Applicants should take note of the following timeline:

  • Priority Application Deadline: August 15, 2026
  • Program Start: September 8, 2026
  • Virtual Summit: January 21, 2027
  • Final Selection Announcement: August 26, 2026

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early application is strongly encouraged.

Eligibility Criteria

The fellowship is designed for individuals who are already making measurable impact in their communities.

Who Can Apply:

  1. Entrepreneurs or Community Leaders
    • Leading initiatives that support community development
    • Addressing education, resilience, or disaster-related challenges
  2. Experience and Impact
    • Ventures operating for 1–5 years
    • Fewer than 10 employees
    • Demonstrated measurable impact
  3. Funding and Traction
    • Secured funding (grants, donations, investments), or
    • Generating revenue
  4. Commitment
    • Must fully participate in all program activities
  5. Age Requirement
    • Must be 18 years or older

Eligible Countries

Applicants are encouraged to apply if they are creating impact in:

  • Germany
  • Mexico
  • South Africa
  • Thailand
  • Brazil
  • Vietnam

Priority is given to those working in Ford plant communities and regions affected by recurring disasters.

Check the full eligibility requirements HERE.

Fellowship Impact and Track Record

Since its inception, the program has achieved remarkable results:

  • Supported 99 Fellows
  • Impacted over 4,600 entrepreneurs
  • Created 2,435 jobs
  • Raised over $18.6 million in capital

This demonstrates the fellowship’s strong commitment to real-world impact and community transformation.

Why You Should Apply

The Ford Philanthropy Fellowship stands out because it:

  • Is fully funded
  • Focuses on real-world community impact
  • Provides hands-on entrepreneurial training
  • Offers global mentorship and networking
  • Supports long-term leadership development

It is not just a fellowship—it is a platform for building sustainable change.

Application Process

The selection process is structured to identify high-potential leaders:

  1. Online Application
    • Takes approximately 30 minutes
    • Submit before the priority deadline
  2. Application Review
    • Evaluation by the admissions committee
  3. Interview Stage
    • Selected candidates attend a 20-minute virtual interview
  4. Final Selection
    • Results announced by August 26, 2026

Click HERE to start your application!

If you’d like to nominate someone directly, use this link and the team will reach out to them: Here

For more information about the Ford Philanthropy Fellowship 2026, click HERE.

Conclusion

The Ford Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 is a powerful opportunity for entrepreneurs and community leaders to elevate their work, expand their impact, and connect with a global network of changemakers.

If you are committed to strengthening communities and building resilient systems, this fellowship provides the tools, mentorship, and support needed to scale your vision.

Discover more global Fellowship, Leadership Development and Entrepreneurship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Ford Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): Global Opportunity for Entrepreneurs and Community Leaders Driving Impact and Resilience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Program Coordinator/Research Assistant Role at CSIS Americas Program: A Gateway to Policy, Research, and Global Impact
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS), one of the world’s leading bipartisan policy research organizations, has announced an exciting full-time opportunity within its renowned Americas Program. The Program is currently accepting applications for the role of Program Coordinator/Research Assistant (PC/RA)—a dynamic position designed for professionals eager to contribute to impactful policy research and […]

The post Program Coordinator/Research Assistant Role at CSIS Americas Program: A Gateway to Policy, Research, and Global Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS), one of the world’s leading bipartisan policy research organizations, has announced an exciting full-time opportunity within its renowned Americas Program. The Program is currently accepting applications for the role of Program Coordinator/Research Assistant (PC/RA)—a dynamic position designed for professionals eager to contribute to impactful policy research and regional engagement across Latin America and the Caribbean.

This opportunity is ideal for individuals who are highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive, with a strong passion for international affairs and public policy. The selected candidate will play a crucial role in supporting research initiatives, coordinating high-level events, and ensuring the smooth functioning of program operations.

About the Role

The Program Coordinator/Research Assistant position is a hybrid role that combines research, administrative support, and program coordination. It offers hands-on exposure to policy development and international relations, particularly within the context of the Americas.

The role is based in Washington, D.C., placing the candidate at the heart of global policy discourse and institutional collaboration.

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will contribute across multiple functional areas, including:

1. Research and Writing Support

  • Assist in conducting research on topics related to Latin America and the Caribbean
  • Contribute to reports, briefs, and policy papers
  • Support ongoing research projects and data collection

2. Event Coordination

  • Organize and manage both public and private events
  • Coordinate logistics such as scheduling, invitations, and venue arrangements
  • Assist in stakeholder engagement and follow-ups

3. Communications and Outreach

  • Support the development of communication materials
  • Assist in outreach efforts to expand program visibility
  • Collaborate with internal teams to maintain consistent messaging

4. Administrative and Operational Support

  • Provide day-to-day administrative assistance
  • Ensure smooth workflow and operational efficiency
  • Manage documentation, scheduling, and coordination tasks

Location and Work Environment

  • Location: Washington, D.C.
  • Work within a fast-paced, collaborative policy environment
  • Engage with experts, policymakers, and researchers

Language Requirements

  • Spanish fluency is required
  • Portuguese proficiency is considered an advantage

These language skills are essential given the program’s regional focus on Latin America and the Caribbean.

Who Should Apply

This role is best suited for candidates who:

  • Thrive in a fast-paced and intellectually stimulating environment
  • Possess strong organizational and multitasking abilities
  • Have excellent research and writing skills
  • Are proactive and capable of working both independently and collaboratively

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

  • Gain hands-on experience in a globally recognized think tank
  • Work on real-world policy issues impacting the Americas
  • Build a strong professional network in international affairs
  • Develop expertise in research, communications, and program management
  • Contribute to meaningful discussions shaping regional and global policy

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted by May 15, 2026.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the Program Coordinator/Research Assistant Role at CSIS Americas Program.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post Program Coordinator/Research Assistant Role at CSIS Americas Program: A Gateway to Policy, Research, and Global Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Western Union Global Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): Empowering Entrepreneurs Supporting Refugee and Marginalized Communities Worldwide
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Western Union Global Fellowship 2026, powered by the Watson Institute, is a highly competitive, fully funded virtual fellowship designed to support entrepreneurs and leaders working with highly marginalized, refugee, and displaced communities across the globe. This transformative fellowship equips participants with the skills, mentorship, funding strategies, and global networks needed to scale their ventures […]

The post Western Union Global Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): Empowering Entrepreneurs Supporting Refugee and Marginalized Communities Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Western Union Global Fellowship 2026, powered by the Watson Institute, is a highly competitive, fully funded virtual fellowship designed to support entrepreneurs and leaders working with highly marginalized, refugee, and displaced communities across the globe.

This transformative fellowship equips participants with the skills, mentorship, funding strategies, and global networks needed to scale their ventures and create sustainable economic opportunities in underserved regions.

As highlighted in the official toolkit , this program is tailored for impact-driven leaders who are already building solutions and are ready to accelerate their growth and expand their reach.

About the Western Union Global Fellowship

The Western Union Global Fellowship is a 16-week intensive virtual program focused on strengthening ventures that address economic inequality and social challenges affecting displaced and underserved populations.

The fellowship aims to:

  • Equip entrepreneurs with advanced business and leadership skills
  • Strengthen ventures working in fragile and underserved contexts
  • Foster global collaboration among changemakers
  • Drive economic resilience in vulnerable communities

Participants gain access to a structured learning environment combined with hands-on support to scale their impact effectively.

Key Benefits of the Fellowship

The program offers a comprehensive suite of benefits designed to accelerate entrepreneurial growth and impact.

1. Expert-Led Entrepreneurial Training

Fellows receive training in critical areas such as:

  • Product-market fit
  • Data analysis and performance metrics
  • Funding and investment strategies
  • Team management and leadership
  • Pitch development

These sessions are delivered by experienced entrepreneurs and industry experts.

2. World-Class Mentorship

Participants benefit from:

  • Mentorship from globally recognized entrepreneurs and leaders
  • Guidance from fellowship alumni
  • Personalized feedback on business strategies

This mentorship ensures fellows receive practical insights tailored to their ventures.

3. Pitch Coaching and Venture Scaling

The fellowship includes dedicated pitch training to help participants:

  • Refine their storytelling
  • Improve investor readiness
  • Communicate impact effectively

This culminates in a final pitch presentation at the program’s Summit.

4. Basecamp Community Impact Initiative ($1,000 Stipend)

A standout feature of the fellowship is the Basecamp initiative, where fellows:

  • Organize a local community event
  • Engage and support early-stage entrepreneurs
  • Receive a $1,000 stipend to execute their initiative

This ensures fellows create direct impact within their communities.

5. Access to a Global Network

Participants join a powerful ecosystem of:

  • Entrepreneurs
  • Investors
  • Mentors
  • Global partners

This network continues to support fellows beyond the program.

Fellowship Duration and Structure

The Western Union Global Fellowship runs for approximately 16 weeks (4 months) and is fully virtual, making it accessible to participants across different countries.

The program includes:

  • Weekly workshops
  • Mentorship sessions
  • Peer collaboration
  • Venture development activities
  • Final Summit presentation

Important Dates

Applicants should carefully note the following timeline:

  • Application Opens: April 6, 2026
  • Priority Deadline: June 28, 2026
  • Program Start Date: August 11, 2026
  • Final Summit: December 3, 2026

The Summit serves as a culminating event where fellows present their ventures and showcase their progress.

Eligibility Criteria

The fellowship targets entrepreneurs who are already building impactful solutions.

Applicants Must:

  1. Operate a Venture
    • Either nonprofit or for-profit
  2. Stage of Venture
    • At the problem-solution or product-market fit stage
    • Must demonstrate measurable impact
  3. Traction Requirements
    • Generating revenue, or
    • Secured funding (grants, investments, donations), or
    • Built a strong user base
  4. Target Beneficiaries
    • Must serve highly marginalized, refugee, or displaced communities
  5. Commitment
    • Must fully participate in the entire 4-month program

Check full eligibility requirements HERE!

Eligible Countries

Applicants must be creating impact in one or more of the following countries:

  • United States
  • Costa Rica
  • India
  • Philippines
  • Lithuania
  • Argentina
  • Italy
  • Brazil
  • Peru

Fellowship Cohort Size

The program is highly selective, with approximately:

  • 20 Fellows selected globally

This ensures a personalized and high-impact learning experience.

How to Apply

Applicants can apply directly through the official fellowship platform:

  • Application Portal: https://watson.is/western-union-global-fellowship-application/
  • Program Page: https://watson.is/western-union-global-fellowship

Nomination Opportunity

In addition to direct applications, individuals can nominate entrepreneurs.

  • A nomination link is available for referring candidates
  • Selected nominees will be contacted by the fellowship team

This expands access to deserving leaders who may not apply independently.

Why You Should Apply

The Western Union Global Fellowship stands out because it:

  • Fully funds participation
  • Focuses on real-world impact
  • Supports underserved communities
  • Provides practical entrepreneurial tools
  • Connects you to a global ecosystem

It is not just a learning program—it is a platform to scale meaningful change.

Contact Information

For inquiries or further details:

  • Contact Person: Sandra Mancia
  • Email: Sandra@Watson.is

Click HERE to learn more about the Western Union Global Fellowship 2026.

Conclusion

The Western Union Global Fellowship 2026 is a powerful opportunity for entrepreneurs dedicated to solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

By combining mentorship, funding support, training, and global exposure, the program empowers fellows to build sustainable solutions that uplift marginalized and displaced communities.

If you are ready to scale your impact and join a global network of changemakers, this fellowship is designed for you.

Discover more global Fellowship, Entrepreneurship and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY.

The post Western Union Global Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): Empowering Entrepreneurs Supporting Refugee and Marginalized Communities Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
